title: Fixing Invalid Node Deletion in a Binary Tree

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a binary tree (represented as an array of nodes) where each node is an object with two properties: value and children.
statement.outputFormat: Output should be true if the deletion of the node with the given value is successful and is not null, otherwise false.

examples.explanation: Deletion of node with value 1 is successful
Deletion of a node with no children

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution {public:bool deleteNode(TreeNode* root, int val){if (root == NULL) return false; if (root->val == val) {root = NULL; return true;} return deleteNode(root->left, val) || deleteNode(root->right, val);}
buggyStarterCode.java: public boolean deleteNode(TreeNode root, int val){if (root == null) return false; if (root.val == val) {root = null; return true;} return deleteNode(root.left, val) || deleteNode(root.right, val);}
buggyStarterCode.javascript: var deleteNode = function(root, val){if (!root) return false;if (root.val == val){root = null;return true;}return deleteNode(root.left, val) || deleteNode(root.right, val);}
